name: Philippines
conservationEfforts: Philippine Eagle Foundation breeds and releases eagles. Mount Kitanglad and Mount Apo are protected. Community-based forest management covers 5 million hectares. National Greening Program plants trees on degraded lands.
description:
The Philippines, an archipelago of over 7,600 islands in Southeast Asia, is one of the world's most biologically rich and threatened regions. With only 7% of original habitat remaining, the Philippines faces dire conservation challenges.

Despite severe habitat loss, the islands harbor astonishing levels of endemism: 66% of plant species and 41% of vertebrates are found nowhere else. Each major island has evolved its own endemic species. The Philippine eagle, the world's largest eagle, is the national bird and a powerful conservation symbol. The islands also host the world's largest flower, Rafflesia, and smallest primate, the tarsier.
